name: Dandelion Jelly
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick bright, fresh dandelion blossoms and pack them tightly in the quart container.
2. Rinse quickly in cold water to remove any insects or dirt; do not leave them in the water for long as they may become damaged.
3. Pull up a comfortable chair, then snip off the stems and green collars so only the petals remain (this can take about four hours).
4. In an enamel saucepan, boil the dandelion petals in water for 3–4 minutes until the water takes on their color.
5. Cool and strain the petals, pressing them with your fingers to extract all the juice; alternatively, line a sieve with moistened cheesecloth and strain it.
6. Measure out 3 cups of dandelion liquid. Add the lemon juice and fruit pectin; stir until combined.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil in a large kettle, then add the sugar and stir well. Boil for 2 ½ minutes.
8. Pour into hot sterilized jelly jars and seal. Process the jars for five minutes in a boiling water bath.
Yield: Five ½‑pint jars.
